politician.profile_narrative: John Cornyn represents Texas in the U.S. Senate since December 2002. He was the lead Senate Republican negotiator on Bipartisan Safer Communities Act (June 2022).

promises.verdict_reasoning: Lead Senate Republican negotiator on BSCA after Uvalde shooting in Texas. Voted YES (one of 15 Republicans).
Voted YES on the Tax Cuts and Jobs Act (December 2017). Has consistently supported TCJA-extension framework.
Voted YES on Justices Gorsuch (2017), Kavanaugh (2018), and Barrett (2020). Dobbs (2022) overturned Roe with all three in the majority.

Voted YES on the 2017 'skinny repeal' (failed 49-51, McCain thumbs-down). Per /methodology, when the politician voted yes but the bill failed by a single dissenting vote from their own party, that's PARTIAL rather than BROKEN.
Voted NO on the Women's Health Protection Act. Supported confirmation of pro-life justices who overturned Roe.
Voted NO on most federal gun-control measures during tenure. NRA rating A or A+.
Voted YES on H.R. 2 framework. Position on the February 2024 Bipartisan Border Bill varied; comprehensive enforcement legislation did not pass during the Biden administration.
Voted to expedite energy permitting and against Biden-era leasing restrictions. U.S. became net energy exporter (2019).

promises.promise_text: Hold the line on federal spending.
promises.verdict_reasoning: Voted YES on TCJA (CBO-projected $1.9T deficit) and CARES Act ($2.2T). National debt rose substantially during tenure.
Voted NO on the Respect for Marriage Act (citing religious-liberty concerns) and supported religious-liberty appropriations provisions.
Voted YES on Israel aid packages. Co-sponsored multiple Israel-support measures.
Voted yes on Trump nominees including AG, SecDef, DNI, Treasury during the 119th Congress.
Voted no on most Biden judicial nominees in minority. Could not block confirmation of Justice Ketanji Brown Jackson (April 2022, 53-47).

promises.promise_text: Withdraw federal support for sanctuary cities.
promises.verdict_reasoning: Multiple bills introduced restricting federal funds to sanctuary jurisdictions; none reached final passage during the term.

politician.profile_narrative: Mark Kelly represents Arizona in the U.S. Senate since December 2020. He was widely speculated as Harris's 2024 VP pick (Walz was selected).

promises.verdict_reasoning: Voted YES on BSCA. Founded gun-safety advocacy group with his wife Gabby Giffords after her 2011 shooting.
Voted YES on the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act (IIJA, November 2021).
Voted YES on the Inflation Reduction Act (August 2022), $369B in climate spending.
Voted YES on IRA Medicare drug-pricing negotiation provisions.

promises.promise_text: Restore voting rights / John Lewis VRA.
promises.verdict_reasoning: Voted YES on cloture; failed 49-51 due to Republican filibuster + Manchin/Sinema opposition to rule change. Per /methodology, blocked-by-Congress when politician took maximally available action routes to PARTIAL.
Voted YES on Women's Health Protection Act cloture; failed 49-51 (Manchin NO).
Voted YES on Bipartisan Safer Communities Act (June 2022). Assault-weapons ban did not pass.
Voted YES on Justice Ketanji Brown Jackson + the majority of lower-court Biden nominees.
Voted YES on every major Ukraine aid package 2022-2024.
Cloture failed Republican filibuster. Respect for Marriage Act passed (signed December 2022).
Supported Biden's broad cancellation (SCOTUS-struck-down). Approximately $138B in narrower cancellations approved.

promises.promise_text: Raise the federal minimum wage to $15.
promises.verdict_reasoning: The Raise the Wage Act of 2021 (included in early ARP drafts) was stripped on Byrd-rule objection. No standalone vote reached the floor.

House-passed BBB included full SALT restoration; killed in Senate. Cap remains at $10k through TCJA expiry. Per /methodology, blocked-by-Senate when politician took maximally available action routes to PARTIAL.
IRA imposed 15% corporate minimum tax + 1% stock-buyback excise. Largest corporate tax hike since the 1986 TRA.

politician.name: Byron Lowell Donalds
politician.professional_background: Former Florida state representative

promises.verdict_reasoning: Voted YES on the Tax Cuts and Jobs Act (December 2017). Has consistently supported TCJA-extension framework.
Voted YES on Justices Gorsuch (2017), Kavanaugh (2018), and Barrett (2020). Dobbs (2022) overturned Roe with all three in the majority.

Voted YES on the 2017 'skinny repeal' (failed 49-51, McCain thumbs-down). Per /methodology, when the politician voted yes but the bill failed by a single dissenting vote from their own party, that's PARTIAL rather than BROKEN.
Voted NO on the Women's Health Protection Act. Supported confirmation of pro-life justices who overturned Roe.
Voted NO on most federal gun-control measures during tenure. NRA rating A or A+.
Voted YES on H.R. 2 framework. Position on the February 2024 Bipartisan Border Bill varied; comprehensive enforcement legislation did not pass during the Biden administration.
Voted to expedite energy permitting and against Biden-era leasing restrictions. U.S. became net energy exporter (2019).

promises.promise_text: Hold the line on federal spending.
promises.verdict_reasoning: Voted YES on TCJA (CBO-projected $1.9T deficit) and CARES Act ($2.2T). National debt rose substantially during tenure.
Voted NO on the Respect for Marriage Act (citing religious-liberty concerns) and supported religious-liberty appropriations provisions.
Voted YES on Israel aid packages. Co-sponsored multiple Israel-support measures.
Voted yes on Trump nominees including AG, SecDef, DNI, Treasury during the 119th Congress.
Voted no on most Biden judicial nominees in minority. Could not block confirmation of Justice Ketanji Brown Jackson (April 2022, 53-47).

promises.promise_text: Withdraw federal support for sanctuary cities.
promises.verdict_reasoning: Multiple bills introduced restricting federal funds to sanctuary jurisdictions; none reached final passage during the term.

promises.verdict_reasoning: Voted YES on the Tax Cuts and Jobs Act (December 2017). Has consistently supported TCJA-extension framework.
Voted YES on Justices Gorsuch (2017), Kavanaugh (2018), and Barrett (2020). Dobbs (2022) overturned Roe with all three in the majority.

Voted YES on the 2017 'skinny repeal' (failed 49-51, McCain thumbs-down). Per /methodology, when the politician voted yes but the bill failed by a single dissenting vote from their own party, that's PARTIAL rather than BROKEN.
Voted NO on the Women's Health Protection Act. Supported confirmation of pro-life justices who overturned Roe.
Voted NO on most federal gun-control measures during tenure. NRA rating A or A+.
Voted YES on H.R. 2 framework. Position on the February 2024 Bipartisan Border Bill varied; comprehensive enforcement legislation did not pass during the Biden administration.
Voted to expedite energy permitting and against Biden-era leasing restrictions. U.S. became net energy exporter (2019).

promises.promise_text: Hold the line on federal spending.
promises.verdict_reasoning: Voted YES on TCJA (CBO-projected $1.9T deficit) and CARES Act ($2.2T). National debt rose substantially during tenure.
Voted NO on the Respect for Marriage Act (citing religious-liberty concerns) and supported religious-liberty appropriations provisions.
Voted YES on Israel aid packages. Co-sponsored multiple Israel-support measures.
Voted yes on Trump nominees including AG, SecDef, DNI, Treasury during the 119th Congress.
Voted no on most Biden judicial nominees in minority. Could not block confirmation of Justice Ketanji Brown Jackson (April 2022, 53-47).

promises.promise_text: Withdraw federal support for sanctuary cities.

promises.verdict_reasoning: Voted YES on the Tax Cuts and Jobs Act (December 2017). Has consistently supported TCJA-extension framework.

Voted YES on the 2017 'skinny repeal' (failed 49-51, McCain thumbs-down). Per /methodology, when the politician voted yes but the bill failed by a single dissenting vote from their own party, that's PARTIAL rather than BROKEN.
Voted NO on the Women's Health Protection Act. Supported confirmation of pro-life justices who overturned Roe.
Voted NO on most federal gun-control measures during tenure. NRA rating A or A+.
Voted YES on H.R. 2 framework. Position on the February 2024 Bipartisan Border Bill varied; comprehensive enforcement legislation did not pass during the Biden administration.
Voted to expedite energy permitting and against Biden-era leasing restrictions. U.S. became net energy exporter (2019).

promises.promise_text: Hold the line on federal spending.

Voted YES on Israel aid packages. Co-sponsored multiple Israel-support measures.
Voted yes on Trump nominees including AG, SecDef, DNI, Treasury during the 119th Congress.
Voted no on most Biden judicial nominees in minority. Could not block confirmation of Justice Ketanji Brown Jackson (April 2022, 53-47).

promises.promise_text: Withdraw federal support for sanctuary cities.

promises.promise_text: Raise the federal minimum wage to $15.
promises.verdict_reasoning: The Raise the Wage Act of 2021 (included in early ARP drafts) was stripped on Byrd-rule objection. No standalone vote reached the floor.

IRA imposed 15% corporate minimum tax + 1% stock-buyback excise. Largest corporate tax hike since the 1986 TRA.

politician.name: Phil King
politician.professional_background: TX State Representative (HD-61) 1999-2025; passed multiple election-integrity bills

promises.verdict_reasoning: Co-authored SB 1, the Texas Election Integrity Protection Act, signed Sep 7, 2021. Tightened mail-voting ID, prohibited drive-through voting, expanded poll-watcher access.

Voted NO on the Women's Health Protection Act. Supported confirmation of pro-life justices who overturned Roe.

promises.promise_text: Hold the line on federal spending.

politician.name: Peter Allen Stauber
politician.professional_background: House Natural Resources subcommittee chair

promises.promise_text: Open Boundary Waters area to mining.
promises.verdict_reasoning: Mining lease cancellation upheld in federal court 2024; congressional reversal did not advance.
Voted YES on the Tax Cuts and Jobs Act (December 2017). Has consistently supported TCJA-extension framework.
Voted YES on Justices Gorsuch (2017), Kavanaugh (2018), and Barrett (2020). Dobbs (2022) overturned Roe with all three in the majority.

Voted YES on the 2017 'skinny repeal' (failed 49-51, McCain thumbs-down). Per /methodology, when the politician voted yes but the bill failed by a single dissenting vote from their own party, that's PARTIAL rather than BROKEN.
Voted NO on the Women's Health Protection Act. Supported confirmation of pro-life justices who overturned Roe.
Voted NO on most federal gun-control measures during tenure. NRA rating A or A+.
Voted YES on H.R. 2 framework. Position on the February 2024 Bipartisan Border Bill varied; comprehensive enforcement legislation did not pass during the Biden administration.
Voted to expedite energy permitting and against Biden-era leasing restrictions. U.S. became net energy exporter (2019).

promises.promise_text: Hold the line on federal spending.
promises.verdict_reasoning: Voted YES on TCJA (CBO-projected $1.9T deficit) and CARES Act ($2.2T). National debt rose substantially during tenure.
Voted NO on the Respect for Marriage Act (citing religious-liberty concerns) and supported religious-liberty appropriations provisions.
Voted YES on Israel aid packages. Co-sponsored multiple Israel-support measures.
Voted yes on Trump nominees including AG, SecDef, DNI, Treasury during the 119th Congress.
Voted no on most Biden judicial nominees in minority. Could not block confirmation of Justice Ketanji Brown Jackson (April 2022, 53-47).

promises.promise_text: Withdraw federal support for sanctuary cities.

politician.name: Wesley P. Hunt

promises.verdict_reasoning: Voted yes on Lower Energy Costs Act (H.R.1, passed House 225-204 March 30, 2023). Senate did not take up.
Voted YES on the Tax Cuts and Jobs Act (December 2017). Has consistently supported TCJA-extension framework.
Voted YES on Justices Gorsuch (2017), Kavanaugh (2018), and Barrett (2020). Dobbs (2022) overturned Roe with all three in the majority.

Voted YES on the 2017 'skinny repeal' (failed 49-51, McCain thumbs-down). Per /methodology, when the politician voted yes but the bill failed by a single dissenting vote from their own party, that's PARTIAL rather than BROKEN.
Voted NO on the Women's Health Protection Act. Supported confirmation of pro-life justices who overturned Roe.
Voted NO on most federal gun-control measures during tenure. NRA rating A or A+.
Voted YES on H.R. 2 framework. Position on the February 2024 Bipartisan Border Bill varied; comprehensive enforcement legislation did not pass during the Biden administration.
Voted to expedite energy permitting and against Biden-era leasing restrictions. U.S. became net energy exporter (2019).

promises.promise_text: Hold the line on federal spending.
promises.verdict_reasoning: Voted YES on TCJA (CBO-projected $1.9T deficit) and CARES Act ($2.2T). National debt rose substantially during tenure.
Voted NO on the Respect for Marriage Act (citing religious-liberty concerns) and supported religious-liberty appropriations provisions.
Voted YES on Israel aid packages. Co-sponsored multiple Israel-support measures.
Voted yes on Trump nominees including AG, SecDef, DNI, Treasury during the 119th Congress.
Voted no on most Biden judicial nominees in minority. Could not block confirmation of Justice Ketanji Brown Jackson (April 2022, 53-47).
